pnpm.//trustPolicy: Refuse install if a package's trust level decreased from a prior version (e.g. previously had provenance, new version doesn't). Catches the common compromise pattern where an attacker republishes from a stolen account without the original publisher's signing setup. (pnpm >= 10.21)
pnpm.//blockExoticSubdeps: Refuse transitive deps from non-registry sources (git URLs, tarballs, local paths). Direct deps can still use exotic sources when explicitly declared, but a transitive dep is never allowed to drag in code from outside the registry. (pnpm >= 10.26)
